description_long: This flower features a broad, irregularly shaped, pale petal structure with a subtle gradient from white at the edges to a faint greenish hue near the center. The central disk is a prominent yellow, slightly raised and textured, resembling the reproductive core of a typical angiosperm. The petals appear thin and delicate, with gentle undulations and a somewhat crumpled surface, giving a natural, organic look. There are no visible leaves or stem attached to the flower, and the underside is dark, suggesting shadow or a thin, papery structure. The overall form is flat and wide, with the petal edges uneven and asymmetrical. This flower would likely be found as a decorative or natural element in urban parks, gardens, or landscaped areas.
description: A wide, pale flower with an irregular, crumpled petal structure and a prominent yellow central disk. The petals fade from white to greenish near the center, with no visible stem or leaves.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full, irregular white petal spread and the yellow central disk.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the gentle undulations and thinness of the petals, with the yellow center visible.
description_view_2: Edge-on view revealing the extreme thinness and flatness of the flower, with the underside appearing dark.
description_view_3: Opposite angled view showing the petal texture and the raised yellow center from a different perspective.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, spreading outward from the central disk with irregular, overlapping edges and no clear symmetry.
